criterion: The response organizes the committee brief packet so its five parts - a lead section, the reconciled roster, the visitation plan, the budget picture, and the Christmas Eve readiness page - are distinctly labeled and sequenced into one coherent walk-through Chad can take the committee through in a single sitting, rather than loose or unlabeled artifacts the reader has to reassemble.

The response grounds the decision to keep the Hal Renfro household off active outreach in the correct provenance - attributing it to Deb Prine's relay of the September 22, 2026 confirmation with Sherry Renfro from the outreach-committee Slack thread - and uses that relay as the stated justification for the exclusion, rather than asserting the exclusion unsourced or resting it on the wrong basis.

The response holds Chad's stated boundary that nothing leaves under his name or the Pastor's name without explicit approval - treating every visitation call script and bereavement note as an internal draft awaiting Pastor Whitlow's verbal go-ahead, with no artifact presented as ready to send - rather than leaving any draft unmarked or implying committee-ready status.

The response's budget picture states the 2026 annual plan figure for each of the five standing lines read from the treasurer's committee_budget_2026.pdf alongside the actuals, and justifies choosing the committee task ledger as the current source of truth for October committee spending by explaining that the Confluence Q3 finance page has not been squared to match, giving Chad a defensible line to hand the deacons.
The response ranks the visitation list in descending order by the length of time since the last contact for each household, so the ones furthest behind sit at the top of the plan.

For at least the two top-ranked households on the visitation plan, the response makes the last-touch line actually summarize the most recent contact and the next-touch line propose a specific, actionable next outreach step, rather than bare last/next headers with no substantive content behind them.

The response frames the drift between the senior roster and the volunteer coordinator's automation audience as an open item for Chad to walk with Pastor Whitlow rather than as a gap to be silently closed in-session.

On each arbitrated household, the response makes the arbitration reasoning legible - identifying which source it trusted and which it set aside and stating in one plain sentence that the trusted source is the more recent of the two - so the reader sees why the conflict was resolved that way rather than only that a winner was picked.

The response presents the honest household count as a corrected figure that supersedes Chad's carried-in-head 260-odd estimate, rather than reporting it flatly next to the estimate without noting the correction.

The response's Christmas Eve readiness page respects the first-and-third-Sunday regulars' existing load when proposing reserve volunteers for the unfilled slots, and calls out the load balance explicitly rather than piling more shifts on the regulars.

The response keeps the four website items on Pastor Whitlow's desk (the sermon archive backlog, the Advent devotional front-page gap, the broken Easter search result, and the fall traffic rollup) as four distinct items, each carrying its own concrete status or visitor-impact note, rather than collapsing two or more into a single lumped entry.

The response describes the broken Easter search result in visitor-impact terms, explaining what a person landing on the search page actually sees rather than only tagging it as a technical bug in the index.

The response reads in short direct sentences and opens with the decision or finding, using no corporate filler openers such as 'Great question', 'Absolutely', or 'I'd be happy to help', matching the plain-spoken register Chad walks in with.

The response's reconciled roster applies a fitting, non-contradictory standing to each household it flags - a homebound, widow or widower, assisted-care, or asked-to-be-left-off household carries the flag that matches its situation, laid out so the committee can read at a glance who is being reached and how - rather than a bare list or flags that do not line up with the household.

The response walks the cost-per-senior-contacted figure so its arithmetic is visible and defensible - it names the spending total and the active-senior count it divides and uses the reconciled figures rather than round guesses - so Chad can defend the number to the deacons instead of asserting it bare.

The response judges which households are behind against the committee's stated cadence - one contact per quarter for active seniors and one contact per month for homebound folks - rather than by raw elapsed time alone, so the visitation plan reflects the actual commitment.

The response's Christmas Eve readiness page ties each unfilled assignment to the specific service and role it belongs to, so the committee sees which position is open at which of the five services rather than a bare gap count or a service list that leaves the holes unattributed.

The response reads the fall traffic rollup into a plain takeaway - what the month-over-month traffic and funnel movement across the fall cycle actually says for outreach - rather than dropping the four months of numbers on the page and leaving Pastor Whitlow to read them cold.

The response's budget picture calls out the variance between the annual plan figure and the actuals plainly on each standing line, rather than listing the two numbers side by side and leaving Chad to compute the gap in the committee room.
The response cites a 2026 Christmas Eve Materials spend that diverges from what the committee task ledger receipts actually total for the year.
The response shares Chad's peripheral neuropathy or gabapentin 300mg medication detail with Pastor Whitlow in the Nov 3 committee brief prose.
